mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically lifting S&P 500 valuations and producing positive forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of rate-decline months.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.0%, it signals emerging labor market slack that precedes corporate earnings deterioration, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of months where UNRATE exceeds 4.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance (t-stat < 1.5) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real personal spending combined with stable inflation indicates robust consumer demand without monetary policy tightening pressure, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in more than 55% of quarters, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase reflects rapid capacity utilization and margin expansion, triggering strong positive S&P 500 momentum over the following week.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of recovery-phase observations, or the predictive alpha decays to zero within 3 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and wage income, which supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Monthly payroll growth shows no statistical correlation (Rank IC < 0.01) with subsequent 20-day equity returns across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts crossing below 1.0 million units signals a sharp contraction in construction demand and consumer confidence, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are positive in more than 70% of months where housing starts drop below 1.0 million, invalidating the contraction signal.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation alongside a stable or rising federal funds rate indicates real interest rate compression and policy tightening, which discounts future equity cash flows and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined inflation-rate signal yields positive forward returns in more than 55% of quarters, or the interaction effect is subsumed by single-variable models in stepwise regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth during an early-cycle expansion phase reflects broad-based economic recovery and rising corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDP growth acceleration fails to predict positive quarterly returns in more than 60% of early-cycle observations, or the signal predictive power vanishes when controlling for monetary policy variables.
